title: ProDec Advance 24mm (1 inch) x 50m Low Tack Precision Edge Multi Surface Painters Masking Tape for Razor Sharp Lines with No Paint Bleed For Indoor Painting and Decorating Compatible with All Paints
features: 1" (24mm) width x 50m length trade quality masking tape made from easy tear Japanese rice paper for excellent barrier properties, eliminating paint bleed for razor sharp lines
High tech, low tack, low residue adhesive for smooth application and release
Ideal for walls, wood, metal, glass, mouldings, switches, sockets and more
Low tack adhesion grade ideal for newly painted and delicate surfaces where gentle adhesion is required without surface damage
Test a small area first and remove ideally before paint dries (max 24 hours after application)
MORE INFORMATION BELOW in the 'From The Manufacturer' section - click on 'See More Product Details' or scroll down
description: From the manufacturer ProDec is the UK's biggest and most widely distributed brand of trade decorating accessories, so whether you're a seasoned pro, enthusiastic DIYer or even a beginner, we've got everything you need for a great finish - just add paint Precision Edge - for ultra sharp painting lines Precision Edge Masking Tape makes achieving razor paint sharp lines simple, protecting surfaces quickly and easily and preventing unwanted paint marks, flicks and splashes. It's made using Japanese rice paper that has long, strong fibres that give excellent barrier properties and eliminate paint bleed for the crispest of edges, whilst being easy to tear, enabling quick and convenient coverage of both long and short runs. The paper is coated with a high tech adhesive that gives even application when put down and smooth release when peeled, leaving no residue. Precision Edge is available in a range of grades to suit the job at hand Standard grade Ideal for bare and previously painted surfaces where the paint has fully cured (more than 28 days). Low Tack grade A lower strength adhesive than Standard grade, ideal for newly painted (less than 28 days) and delicate surfaces where gentle adhesion is required without surface damage. UV Resistant grade Ideal for hot spots such as conservatories, south facing windows, etc. to prevent excessive adhesion or 'baking on' caused by heat. Low tack makes it ideal for newly painted and delicate surfaces. Masking tape application guidelines Follow these simple guidelines to get the best results with masking tape: Always try a small, unobtrusive area first to check compatibility with the surface - it will save potential time and hassle later For delicate or newly painted surfaces (less than 28 days) use a Low Tack grade to avoid surface damage when peeling For hot spots such as conservatories, south facing windows, etc. use a UV Resistant grade to prevent excessive adhesion caused by heat Apply the tape with even but not excessive pressure - the tape will do its job and you'll find it easier to remove without the risk of over-adhesion If possible, remove the tape before the paint dries - this prevents any paint skin forming over the join between the tape and the painted surface Some wallpapers such as painted or highly patterned papers may not be suitable for masking tape - always test first and don't use excessive pressure
